# Koin Compiler Plugin — Kotlin Compiler Plugin for Koin DI

> Native Kotlin Compiler Plugin (K2) for the Koin dependency injection framework. Transforms DSL calls (`single<T>()`, `factory<T>()`, ...) and processes annotations (`@Singleton`, `@Factory`, `@Module`, `@ComponentScan`, ...) at compile time. Adds compile-time graph validation — if it compiles, it works.

## Key Facts

- **Version**: 1.0.0
- **Language**: Kotlin (K2 compiler required, 2.3.x+)
- **Koin compatibility**: 4.2.0-RC2+
- **License**: Apache 2.0
- **Repository**: https://github.com/InsertKoinIO/koin-compiler-plugin
- **Parent project (Koin)**: https://github.com/InsertKoinIO/koin

## Installation

```kotlin
// settings.gradle.kts — Gradle Plugin Portal is enabled by default
// Or add: pluginManagement { repositories { gradlePluginPortal() } }

// build.gradle.kts (per-module, in any module that defines or starts Koin)
plugins {
    id("io.insert-koin.koin-compiler") version "1.0.0"
}

dependencies {
    implementation(platform("io.insert-koin:koin-bom:4.2.0"))
    implementation("io.insert-koin:koin-core")
    // The plugin works with the standard Koin DSL — no extra runtime dep needed.
    // For annotations, also add:
    implementation("io.insert-koin:koin-core-annotations")
}
```

## Plugin Configuration

```kotlin
koinCompiler {
    userLogs = true            // Log discovered components (default: false)
    debugLogs = false          // Verbose internal processing logs
    unsafeDslChecks = true     // Validate create(::T) is the only call in scope { ... } lambdas
    skipDefaultValues = true   // Skip injection for params with Kotlin default values (non-null, unannotated)
    compileSafety = true       // Compile-time dependency graph validation (A2/A3/A4)
    strictSafety = null        // null = auto-detect; force true/false to override
}
```

### Compile Safety Levels

When `compileSafety = true`, the plugin validates the dependency graph at three levels:

- **A2 — Per-Module**: each module's definitions must resolve against its visible scope
- **A3 — Full Graph**: at the aggregator (`startKoin<T>()` / `@KoinApplication`), validates every reachable definition
- **A4 — Call-Site**: every `get<T>()`, `inject<T>()`, `koinViewModel<T>()`, etc. must resolve to a known definition

Works with both the DSL and annotation styles. Replaces runtime `verify()` / `checkModules()`.

### Strict Safety (Incremental Compilation Bypass)

Auto-enabled on modules containing `startKoin`, `koinApplication`, or `@KoinApplication`. Forces the aggregator's `compileKotlin` to re-run every build so full-graph (A3) validation isn't skipped by Kotlin's incremental compiler when DSL lambda bodies or `@ComponentScan` packages change. Override with `strictSafety = true|false`. No effect when `compileSafety = false`.

## Supported DSL Functions

These calls are transformed at compile time. Without the plugin, they don't exist — the plugin generates the wiring.

| Function | Receiver | Generates |
|---|---|---|
| `single<T>()` | `Module` | Singleton from primary constructor |
| `factory<T>()` | `Module` | Factory from primary constructor |
| `viewModel<T>()` | `Module` | ViewModel from primary constructor |
| `worker<T>()` | `Module` | WorkManager Worker from primary constructor |
| `scoped<T>()` | `ScopeDSL` | Scoped from primary constructor |
| `create(::T)` | `Scope` | Constructor reference inside `scope { ... }` |
| `module<T>()` | `KoinApplication` | Loads a single `@Module` class |
| `modules(vararg KClass)` | `KoinApplication` | Loads multiple `@Module` classes |
| `startKoin<T>()` | top-level | Application entry point — triggers full-graph (A3) validation |
| `koinApplication<T>()` | top-level | Same as `startKoin<T>()` but returns the `KoinApplication` |
| `koinConfiguration<T>()` | top-level | KMP-friendly Koin configuration |

### Example

```kotlin
import org.koin.dsl.module
import org.koin.dsl.bind

val appModule = module {
    single<Database>()                                // singleton, auto-wired
    single<UserRepositoryImpl>() bind UserRepository::class
    factory<GetUserUseCase>()                          // factory, auto-wired
    viewModel<HomeViewModel>()
    worker<SyncWorker>()

    scope<UserSession> {
        scoped<UserPreferences>()
    }
}

class MyApp : Application() {
    override fun onCreate() {
        super.onCreate()
        startKoin<MyApp> {                              // <MyApp> triggers full-graph validation
            androidContext(this@MyApp)
            modules(appModule)
        }
    }
}
```

## Annotations

### Definition Annotations

Apply to classes, to functions inside `@Module` classes, or to top-level functions (discovered by `@ComponentScan`).

| Annotation | Generates |
|---|---|
| `@Single` / `@Singleton` | `single { }` |
| `@Factory` | `factory { }` |
| `@Scoped` | `scoped { }` |
| `@KoinViewModel` | `viewModel { }` |
| `@KoinWorker` | `worker { }` |

### Module Annotations

| Annotation | Effect |
|---|---|
| `@Module` | Marks a class as a Koin module container |
| `@ComponentScan("pkg")` | Scans package(s) for annotated classes and top-level functions |
| `@Configuration` | Auto-discovered by `@KoinApplication` (supports labels: `@Configuration("test", "prod")`) |
| `@KoinApplication(modules)` | Application entry point — assembles `@Configuration` modules and runs full-graph validation |

### Parameter Annotations

| Annotation | Effect |
|---|---|
| `@Named("x")` | String qualifier → `named("x")` |
| `@Qualifier(name = "x")` | String qualifier → `named("x")` |
| `@Qualifier(MyType::class)` | Type qualifier → `typeQualifier<MyType>()` |
| `@InjectedParam` | Uses `ParametersHolder.get()` |
| `@Property("key")` | Injects a property value (warns if no `@PropertyValue` default) |
| `@PropertyValue("key")` | Provides a default value for `@Property` |
| `@Provided` | Type is supplied externally — skipped during safety validation |
| `@ScopeId(name = "x")` | Resolves from a named scope → `getScope("x").get<T>()` |
| `@ScopeId(MyScope::class)` | Resolves from a typed scope |

### Example

```kotlin
import org.koin.core.annotation.*

@Module
@ComponentScan("com.myapp")
@Configuration
class AppModule

@Singleton
class Database

@Singleton
class UserRepository(private val db: Database)

@KoinViewModel
class HomeViewModel(
    private val repo: UserRepository,
    @InjectedParam val userId: String,
) : ViewModel()

// Top-level provider functions are also discovered by @ComponentScan
@Singleton
fun provideJson(): Json = Json { ignoreUnknownKeys = true }

@KoinApplication
class MyApp : Application() {
    override fun onCreate() {
        super.onCreate()
        startKoin<MyApp> { androidContext(this@MyApp) }
    }
}
```

## Migration from KSP (Koin Annotations)

The compiler plugin replaces the older KSP-based `koin-ksp-compiler`. Annotations stay the same; remove the KSP setup and add the Kotlin compiler plugin instead. See: https://github.com/InsertKoinIO/koin-compiler-plugin/blob/main/docs/MIGRATION_FROM_KSP.md

- **Official Koin docs**: https://insert-koin.io/docs/intro/index
